skill.name: literature-management
skill.description: Import local literature into a managed library with offline deduplication, tagging, and searchable index.
skill.summary: Local literature management tool that imports PDF, BibTeX, RIS, CSV, and TXT files into a structured library with automatic deduplication and tagging.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 95 static scanner findings across 8 files (962 lines). All findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate offline literature management tool. SHA-1 is used for file content deduplication (not security). Shell backtick patterns are bash command examples in documentation markdown code blocks. shutil file operations are core functionality for organizing papers. The hardcoded URL is a source repository citation. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, credential theft, or malicious intent detected.

medium_findings.title: SHA-1 used for file deduplication
medium_findings.description: hash_file() uses hashlib.sha1() for computing file content fingerprints. SHA-1 is cryptographically broken but acceptable for non-security use cases like duplicate detection. The hash is used only as a dedup_key for identifying identical files, not for authentication or integrity verification.

low_findings.title: Shell backtick execution in documentation
low_findings.description: DISMISSED - Bash command examples in SKILL.md and references/examples.md use markdown code blocks with backticks. These are documentation examples showing users how to run the import script, not executable code. Examples include 'python scripts/import_library.py --help' and pip install commands.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL for source repository
low_findings.description: DISMISSED - SKILL.md line 7 contains a URL linking to the source GitHub repository (github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). This is a legitimate source citation, not a network call. The skill explicitly states no network access is used or required.

low_findings.title: shutil file operations for library management
low_findings.description: DISMISSED - shutil.copy2() at line 288 and shutil.move() at line 304 are core functionality for copying and moving imported paper files into the managed library directory. Source and destination paths are controlled by --source-dir and --library-dir CLI arguments. No arbitrary file writes outside the specified library directory.

low_findings.title: Import uuid module for record IDs
low_findings.description: DISMISSED - import uuid at line 7 is a standard Python library import used for generating unique identifiers for imported library records. This is not system reconnaissance. The uuid.uuid4() generates random UUIDs for the id field in each library entry.

content.user_title: Organize local academic papers into a managed library
content.value_statement: Academic researchers and students struggle with scattered downloaded papers in various formats. This skill imports PDF, BibTeX, RIS, CSV, and TXT files into a structured library with automatic deduplication and tagging.
actual_capabilities: Import academic papers in PDF, BibTeX, RIS, CSV, and TXT formats into a structured library
Automatically deduplicate entries by DOI, title and year combination, or file content hash
Organize imported files into folders by publication year and journal name
Apply custom manual tags and auto-derive tags from metadata keywords
Generate and maintain a searchable JSONL index for later retrieval and filtering
Support copy or move modes with dry-run preview to verify actions before execution
limitations: Requires Python 3.9+ and the pypdf library to be installed separately
Cannot fetch missing metadata from online databases or resolve DOIs
Does not support editing or updating entries in an existing library index after import
Plain text file imports produce entries with limited metadata fields and no file organization

use_cases.title: Import papers for a literature review
use_cases.description: A student downloads 50 papers from various journals and needs them organized by year and journal with automatic deduplication to avoid repeated entries.

use_cases.title: Share a lab literature repository
use_cases.description: A lab manager maintains a shared folder where multiple researchers contribute papers, requiring consistent structure and deduplication across contributions.

use_cases.title: Build a personal offline reference collection
use_cases.description: A researcher wants a searchable offline collection of papers without relying on cloud reference managers that require network access.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PDF import with tags
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user importing PDF papers into a new library
prompt_templates.prompt: Import the papers from ~/Downloads/papers into ~/my-library with the tag machine-learning and show a summary when done.

prompt_templates.title: Batch import with move mode
prompt_templates.scenario: Cleaning up Downloads folder by moving papers into the library
prompt_templates.prompt: Import all papers from ~/Downloads into ~/literature-library. Use move mode and scan all subdirectories. Apply the tag to-read to all entries.

prompt_templates.title: Dry-run preview before real import
prompt_templates.scenario: Previewing what will happen before making changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a dry-run import of PDF files from ~/Downloads/papers into ~/literature-library. Show me how many files would be imported and how many would be skipped as duplicates.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-format library initialization
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up a new literature library from mixed file types without auto-tagging
prompt_templates.prompt: Import all research files from ~/research-papers into ~/literature-library. The folder contains PDF, RIS, and BibTeX files. Use recursive mode and disable auto-tags. Add the tag review-2025.

output_examples.input: Import ~/Downloads/papers into ~/my-library with tag cancer-imaging and show a preview before importing
output_examples.output: Dry-run complete. Found 12 PDF files and 3 BibTeX files. Would import 15 new records. 2 files would be skipped as duplicates. New library location: ~/my-library. Run the same command without --dry-run to proceed with the actual import.

output_examples.input: Import all research files from ~/research-papers into ~/literature-library with recursive scanning and move mode
output_examples.output: Import complete. 28 files imported, 3 duplicates skipped, 0 errors. Library organized at ~/literature-library. Index file created at index.jsonl. Files arranged by publication year and journal name.

best_practices: Always run with --dry-run first to preview which files will be imported and which will be skipped as duplicates
Use consistent tag names across imports to make filtering and searching the index easier later
Keep source and library directories on the same drive to avoid cross-filesystem move issues
anti_patterns: Importing the same source directory multiple times without checking the existing index for already-processed files
Using --move mode without a backup when the source files are the only copy of important papers
Importing very large directories with --recursive without running a dry-run preview first

faq.question: What file formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: PDF, BibTeX (.bib), RIS (.ris), CSV (.csv), and plain text (.txt) files.

faq.question: How does deduplication work?
faq.answer: Records are deduplicated by DOI first. If no DOI is found, the title and year are used. As a final fallback, the file content hash is compared.

faq.question: Does this skill access the internet?
faq.answer: No. All processing is done locally. No network access, external APIs, or credentials are used or required.

faq.question: Can I import papers from online databases?
faq.answer: No. You must download papers to a local folder first. This skill only imports from local directories.

faq.question: How are files organized in the library?
faq.answer: Files are stored in a files/Year/Journal directory structure. Unknown values use UnknownYear or UnknownJournal as defaults.

faq.question: What happens if a file name already exists?
faq.answer: A numeric suffix is added to avoid overwriting. For example, paper.pdf becomes paper_1.pdf if paper.pdf already exists.
